tam.subtitle: All APAC knowledge workers at theoretical full ARPU.

sam.subtitle: Filtered to SG + MY + ID mid-market & SMEs we can credibly serve.
filters: SG + MY + ID only (the language and price-tolerance footprint)

stacks.name: Filtered SG + MY + ID workers
stacks.source: ACRA active entity count + DOSM + BPS triangulation

som.subtitle: Realistic 3-year capture under the committed sales model.

stacks.name: Direct + product-led blend
stacks.source: Internal capacity model — 2-rep team plus PLG self-serve

implications.headline: Wedge-first, not scale-first
implications.body: SOM S$4.6M / 3yr is meaningful but nowhere near winner-takes-all. The thesis is to compete on wedge specificity (a SG-mid-market workflow nobody else owns), not on overall APAC scale.

implications.headline: ARPU above S$10/mo erodes the SAM math
implications.body: Every S$2 of ARPU above S$10 cuts the addressable seat count in the same SAM dollar pool by ~20%. Agent 3 should treat S$10 as the anchor and any premium tier as an add-on, not a base price hike.

implications.headline: Country-readiness gates Y2 expansion
implications.body: Indonesia and Vietnam score 2/5 on price tolerance per `adoption_patterns`. Year-1 SOM should be 70%+ SG, with MY + ID treated as Year-2 partner-channel expansions, not direct-sales targets.

market_size.methodology_appendix: TAM = APAC knowledge-worker count (48M, IDC APAC Productivity Software 2025) × note-tool ARPU (S$10/mo, mid-band of competitor list prices) × 12 months. SAM filters to SG + MY + ID mid-market and SME workers (9.2M per ACRA + DOSM + BPS triangulation, English-primary teams) at the same S$10/mo ARPU. SOM is 5% of SAM cumulative over 3 years, reflecting a 2-rep direct sales team layered with PLG self-serve through a freemium funnel. The 5% figure is the empirical median for productivity-tool entrants in this region per pitch-deck benchmark scans (Y Combinator and Antler portfolio reads, 2023-2025).

policies.title: PSG grant for productivity SaaS
policies.body: Up to 50% support for pre-approved productivity tools listed by IMDA.
policies.implication_for_us: IMDA pre-approval halves effective price for SG SMEs, doubling WTP ceiling.

policies.title: PDPA Section 13 amendments
policies.body: Mandatory data breach notification within 72h; SG residency preferred for sensitive categories.
policies.implication_for_us: SG residency becomes a differentiator against global vendors whose default is US/EU.

policies.title: DPP tax treatment of cross-border SaaS
policies.body: 8% withholding on SaaS invoices paid to non-SG vendors for SG corporates.
policies.implication_for_us: Global competitors' effective price is 8% higher for SG corporates; use in sales.

cultural_signals.observation: SG SMEs prefer founder-led WhatsApp support over ticketing systems.
cultural_signals.evidence: 68% of SG SMEs cite WhatsApp as primary B2B channel (ASME 2025 survey).
cultural_signals.implication_for_us: Offer WhatsApp-first support in onboarding; build WhatsApp Business integration.

economic_signals.implication_for_us: Tight budgets; anchor pricing on productivity ROI, not convenience.

adoption_patterns.note: SMEs lag on tool standardisation, not willingness.

trends.title: AI-native note tools becoming default
trends.evidence: Notion AI, Mem.ai, Reflect all ship AI-first; 62% of new paid seats include AI add-on.
trends.implication_for_us: AI integration is table stakes, not differentiator.
